name: OPTIC NERVE SHEATH MENINGIOMA

demographics.Frequency: 2% of space-occupying orbital masses; < 2% of intracranial meningiomas
demographics.Mean age: 49 years; M÷F = 1÷4; slightly more aggressive in children
demographics.Histo: syncytial growth pattern composed of meningioma cells with indistinct cytoplasmic

dominant_finding: tumor enhancement around nonenhancing optic n.:
discriminating_features: tubular (most common) / fusiform / eccentric (excrescentic) thickening of optic nerve
tumor enhancement around nonenhancing optic n.:
"tram-track" configuration on axial CECT = enhancing tumor on either side of spared

general: tubular (most common) / fusiform / eccentric (excrescentic) thickening of optic nerve
CECT: tumor enhancement around nonenhancing optic n.:
"tram-track" configuration on axial CECT = enhancing tumor on either side of spared
ringlike / doughnut configuration on coronal CECT
linear high-attenuation area parallel to optic nerve (= tumor spread along subarachnoid
bone remodeling ± enlargement of optic canal
minimal extension into optic canal (not uncommon)
iso- to slightly hyperintense relative to optic nerve on T2WI
"tram-track" sign = intensely enhancing extrinsic soft-tissue mass surrounding optic nerve

clinical.Rx: surgical intervention to prevent disease dissemination to contralateral eye if disease
